title: sinapis cheiranthos subsp. recurvata
intro: Sinapis cheiranthos subsp. recurvata, also known as wild mustard, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sinapis cheiranthos subsp. recurvata include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
